body.title: Goal: rack devices follow consistent patterns — no bespoke orchestration
body.result: §3.10 documents the pattern; palace node written; device audit table in ToO; GoogleSecretary refactor ticket filed; pattern is the gate for all future devices
body.description:
The rack's current devices are individually good but architecturally inconsistent — each one invented its own dispatch, routing, and handler-registration approach. Granny now has the right shape (registered dispatcher). The goal is to propagate that shape to the rest of the rack so adding a new device means: implement the three stacks, register at startup, done. No changes to any orchestrator.

This is a simplification goal, not a feature goal. The target: any device can be understood in 5 minutes because it follows the same documented pattern. The secondary target: a new kind of agent can join the rack by self-registering, with no changes to existing code — exactly what we just built for worker dispatch.

This goal gates: GoogleSecretary refactor, Librarian routing review, any new device type (workspace, policy, factory). It is the architectural throughline behind the erector-set vision.

**Affected files:** All devices/* (audit + incremental refactor); docs/TheoryOfOperation.md; palace.concepts.* nodes
**Design rules:** Orchestration over monolith (feedback memory). External state principle. No bespoke orchestration in device internals.
**Scope boundary:** In scope: document the pattern (T-registered-dispatcher-pattern), audit all current devices for fit, file per-device refactor tickets. Out of scope: re-implementing all devices in one sprint — this is a goal that spawns targeted S/M tickets.
**Completion criteria:** All rack devices documented in TheoryOfOperation.md with their pattern classification; at least 3 devices confirmed as following the registered-dispatcher shape; GoogleSecretary has a filed refactor ticket; palace concept node exists and is linked from each device's section in ToO.
